Container.Name: Sublarr
Container.Overview:
Self-hosted subtitle manager for anime and media libraries — a *arr-style companion (and Bazarr alternative) that automatically searches, scores and downloads the best subtitle, then gives you tools to edit, sync and convert it. Runs entirely on your LAN; no cloud required.
**What it does:**
- Searches 21 subtitle providers in parallel (AnimeTosho, Jimaku, OpenSubtitles, SubDL, Subscene, Subf2m, Subsource, SubsDump, Addic7ed, BetaSeries, Titlovi, Titrari, TVSubtitles, Gestdown, Kitsunekko, Napisy24, Podnapisi, YIFY, Zimuku, LegendasDivX, TurkceAltyazi) plus embedded subtitle extraction. - ASS-first scoring with format, dialect, sync-quality and uploader-trust weighting; smart de-duplication; per-provider circuit breakers. - Sonarr/Radarr webhook integration (multi-instance), Jellyfin/Emby/Plex/Kodi library refresh, AniDB absolute-episode-order handling. - Standalone mode (no *arr required): watches a folder and reads .nfo metadata. - Subtitle toolbox: Aegisub-class waveform editor, ffsubsync/alass sync, format conversion, an 18-step post-processing pipeline, batch OCR and Whisper fallback. - Optional LLM translation (Ollama / DeepL / Google / LibreTranslate / OpenAI-compatible) — experimental; quality varies.
**Setup:**
Sublarr is self-contained — it stores everything in an embedded SQLite database, so **no separate database container is required**. After the container is healthy, open http://[IP]:[PORT:5765] and the first-run onboarding wizard walks you through language selection, provider keys and automation. Sonarr/Radarr are optional.
Map the **Media Path** to the same library root your Sonarr/Radarr/Jellyfin see, so subtitle files land next to your videos.
**Optional backends:** PostgreSQL and Redis are supported for larger setups via the `SUBLARR_DATABASE_URL` / `SUBLARR_REDIS_URL` variables (advanced) — point them at your own containers. Not needed for normal use.
**Optional translation:** install the Ollama Community App, pull a general model (e.g. `qwen2.5:14b-instruct`), and set the Ollama URL below to `http://host.docker.internal:11434`. Translation is disabled by default.
**Full documentation:** https://sublarr.de/docs/ · **Source:** https://github.com/Abrechen2/sublarr
Container.DonateText: If Sublarr is useful to you

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the Sublarr web UI and API. Swagger UI is available at /api/docs.

Config.Name: Config Path
Config.Description: Persistent application data — SQLite database, plugins, backups and logs. Single volume; nothing else needs to be mounted for normal use.

Config.Name: Media Path
Config.Description: Root of your media library. Must be the same path your Sonarr/Radarr/Jellyfin use so subtitles are written next to the video files.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file ownership. Unraid default is 99 (nobody).

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file ownership. Unraid default is 100 (users).

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: Container timezone, e.g. Europe/Berlin or UTC.

Config.Name: API Key
Config.Description: Optional API key (X-Api-Key header) protecting the API. Strongly recommended if the port is exposed beyond your LAN.

Config.Name: Sonarr URL
Config.Description: Sonarr instance URL for library refresh, e.g. http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:8989. Leave empty for standalone mode.

Config.Name: Sonarr API Key
Config.Description: Sonarr API key (Settings → General in Sonarr).

Config.Name: Radarr URL
Config.Description: Radarr instance URL, e.g. http://YOUR_SERVER_IP:7878. Leave empty for standalone mode.

Config.Name: Radarr API Key
Config.Description: Radarr API key (Settings → General in Radarr).

Config.Name: Target Language
Config.Description: Default target subtitle language (ISO 639-1), e.g. de or en. Configurable per series/film in the UI.

Config.Name: Ollama URL
Config.Description: Optional Ollama server URL for LLM translation, e.g. http://host.docker.internal:11434. Leave empty to disable translation.

Config.Name: Ollama Model
Config.Description: Ollama model used for translation when an Ollama URL is set.

Config.Name: Path Mapping
Config.Description: Remote=Local path mapping for split *arr setups (semicolon-separated), e.g. /data/media=/mnt/user/media.

Config.Name: Database URL
Config.Description: Advanced: PostgreSQL DSN to use Postgres instead of the built-in SQLite, e.g. postgresql://sublarr:PASSWORD@host.docker.internal:5432/sublarr. Leave empty for SQLite (recommended).

Config.Name: Redis URL
Config.Description: Advanced: Redis connection string for the job queue, e.g. redis://host.docker.internal:6379/0. Leave empty to use the in-process queue (recommended).
